latest.title: Share Admission Application & Total Voting Rights
latest.content:
Today 12:35
RNS Number : 9380X
World Chess PLC
24 March 2026
World Chess Plc
("World Chess" or the "Company")
Share Admission Application & Total Voting Rights
World Chess Plc (LSE:CHSS) is pleased to announce that 201,868,099 new ordinary shares of £0.0001 each (the "Subscription Shares") have now been issued in respect of the previously announced subscription agreements.
Application is being made for the 201,868,099 Subscription Shares to be admitted to trading on the Main Market of the London Stock Exchange which is expected to be on or around 30 March 2026. These shares rank pari passu with the existing ordinary shares of the Company. Following the issue of the 201,868,099 Shares, the Company's issued ordinary share capital shall consist of 1,089,806,579 ordinary shares.
This figure of 1,089,806,579 represents the total voting rights in the Company and should be used by shareholders as the denominator for the calculation by which they can determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in, the Company under the Financial Conduct Authority's Disclosure Guidance & Transparency Rules.
World Chess Plc
Ilya Merenzon, CEO
merenzon@worldchess.com
AlbR Capital Limited
Financial Adviser: David Coffman / Dan Harris
+44 (0) 20 7469 0930
Notes to Editors
World Chess (LSE: CHSS) is a London-based chess gaming and entertainment company and Fédération Internationale des Échecs ("FIDE") official commercial partner. World Chess organised the FIDE Championship Matches in the USA and the UK and revolutionised the sport by signing the biggest media partnerships in chess history. World Chess develops Armageddon, the chess league for prime-time television, and operates worldchess.com, the official FIDE-rated gaming platform. More at worldchess.com.

latest.title: Subscription Agreement
latest.content:
Today 09:10
RNS Number : 5361U
World Chess PLC
26 February 2026
26 February 2026
World Chess Plc
("World Chess" or the "Company")
Subscription Agreement
World Chess Plc (LSE: CHSS), the London-listed chess company and official commercial partner of FIDE, is pleased to announce that it has entered a binding subscription arrangement with two of the Company's existing shareholders (together, the "Investors"), raising approximately £1,154,941 (the "Subscription").
Under the terms of the respective agreements, the Company will issue a total of 175,915,198 new ordinary shares of £0.0001 each (the "Subscription Shares") at an issue price of £0.00656533 per share (the "Issue Price"). The Subscription Shares will be allotted and issued fully paid and will rank pari passu with the Company's existing ordinary shares in all respects.
Completion of the Subscription is subject to the shareholder authority to issue shares to be proposed at a general meeting of the Company being held on 18 March 2026. Should such authority not be obtained, the number of shares to be issued to the Investors will be reduced to 127,605,998, being the current remaining share issuance authority.
As part of this transaction, the period for the fulfilment of the conditions of the warrants referred to in the Company's announcement dated 23 July 2025 will be extended by two years, from 31 December 2026 to 31 December 2028.
Ilya Merenzon, Chief Executive Officer of World Chess Plc, commented:
"This new capital allows us to fund our next phase of growth, and the continued commitment of our existing shareholders gives us both the resources and the confidence to deliver on it."
This announcement contains information which, prior to its disclosure, was inside information as stipulated under Regulation 11 of the Market Abuse (Amendment) (EU Exit) Regulations 2019/310 (as amended).
For more information, visit: www.worldchess.com or contact:
World Chess Plc
Ilya Merenzon, CEO
merenzon@worldchess.com
AlbR Capital Limited
Financial Adviser: David Coffman / Dan Harris
+44 (0) 20 7469 0930

latest.title: Board Changes
latest.content:
12 Feb 2026 13:52
RNS Number : 8217S
World Chess PLC
12 February 2026
World Chess Plc
("World Chess" or the "Company" or the "Group")
Board Changes
World Chess (LSE: CHSS), a leading chess organisation that develops and operates worldchess.com, the official online gaming platform of the International Chess Federation (FIDE),
announces that Neil Rafferty has resigned from the Board of Directors, effective from 11 May 2026.
Mr Rafferty has given three months' notice in accordance with his contractual obligations and will continue to fulfil his duties as Interim Chair and Director until 11 May 2026, or until a new Chairman is appointed, whichever occurs first.
Statement by the Board
The Board would like to express its sincere gratitude to Neil for his valuable contribution and dedicated service to World Chess since the Company's IPO. Neil has played an important role during a significant period in the Company's development, and his insights and commitment have been greatly appreciated.The Board thanks Neil for his service and wish him every success in his future endeavours.
Chairman Succession
The Company has commenced a formal process to appoint a new Chair at the earliest opportunity.
This announcement contains information which, prior to its disclosure, was inside information as stipulated under Regulation 11 of the Market Abuse (Amendment) (EU Exit) Regulations 2019/310 (as amended).
For more information, visit:
www.worldchess.com
or contact:
World Chess
Ilya Merenzon, CEO
merenzon@worldchess.com
AlbR Capital Limited - Financial Adviser
David Coffman / Dan Harris
+44 (0) 20 7469 0930
Notes to Editors
About World Chess Plc
World Chess (LSE: CHSS) is a leading chess organisation that develops and operates worldchess.com, a subscription platform with over one million users and the official online platform of the International Chess Federation (FIDE). The Company organized several World Chess Championships, including in New York and London. It organises the Armageddon Series and produces The World Chess Show, distributed across more than 50 international markets. More at worldchess.com.
This information is provided by RNS, the news service of the London Stock Exchange. RNS is approved by the Financial Conduct Authority to act as a Primary Information Provider in the United Kingdom. company_30s.what_they_do: World Chess Plc runs a global network of chess clubs, online training platforms, and event sponsorships, turning the ancient game into a digital, subscription‑based ecosystem.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Chess is a billion‑dollar pastime that now lives on mobile and streaming. The company’s tech stack lets it reach millions of players worldwide, monetising through lesson packages, tournaments, and branded content.
company_30s.current_state: The firm is in a cash‑burn phase, having just filed a share‑admission application and a subscription agreement to raise fresh capital. It’s still pre‑revenue for many of its digital ventures and is leaning on the chess‑community brand to drive growth.

rns_narrative: The latest filings show the company is actively seeking to raise equity through a share‑admission and a subscription deal, signalling a need for liquidity to fund its digital expansion.
Board changes and a recent general‑meeting result suggest a re‑organisation aimed at streamlining governance and aligning shareholder interests.
The company’s announcement that its flagship show is moving to a mainstream media outlet (BILD) hints at a strategic push to broaden brand visibility and attract new users.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Silent skepticism – the community is watching but not talking.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A few traders see the subscription round as a sign of confidence; they argue the new capital could power a big push into international markets.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Most chatter is hostile: investors fear the company can’t turn a profit and the recent share dilution will wipe out existing holdings.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The mood remains flat – no new voices have emerged since the March filings, keeping the narrative stagnant.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends are flat – no recent spikes, so a surprise catalyst could still deliver a shock move.

near_term_catalysts: If the subscription round closes and the company reports a positive cash flow in the next earnings release, that could serve as a trigger for a bounce.
A successful partnership or a major tournament broadcast deal announced by the end of Q2 would provide a clear catalyst.
A spike in daily trading volume above the 20‑day average, coupled with a rebound to the 200‑day moving average, would signal a technical recovery.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sudden lift in the share price that breaks the recent 200‑day support, a jump in on‑balance‑volume, and a formal announcement of the subscription close. Any sign of the company cutting costs or securing a new revenue stream will be a green flag.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early capitulation phase – the price has dropped sharply, volume has thinned, and sentiment is quiet. The APEX score (high compression) indicates the market is primed for a reversal once a catalyst arrives.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If the price is 80% down from its peak, a 50% bounce would still deliver a 1.4× return. Given the recent equity raise, the firm could soon have enough capital to scale its platform, pushing the price back toward mid‑range levels. Historical RED‑level recoveries average 160% when institutional accumulation follows a crash, so a similar rebound is plausible.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The company is still pre‑profit and has a thin cash cushion. If the subscription deal fails or a key partnership falls through, the stock could fall another 20–30% before stabilising. A delisting risk exists if the share admission fails to meet regulatory thresholds.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential loss: 80% of the current price if the company collapses. Potential gain: 120% if it recovers to the 50% of its all‑time high. The upside is 1.5× the downside – a classic asymmetrical trade.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a break of the 200‑day moving average with a 2‑day confirmation. Size the position to risk only 2% of the portfolio per trade, and set a stop just below the 200‑day support. Use a trailing stop that locks in 25% of the upside once the price hits 20% above the entry.
playbook.if_watching: Hold until the subscription round closes or a partnership deal is signed. If volume surges to 1.5× the 20‑day average and the price crosses the 200‑day MA, consider a partial entry at 75% of the theoretical upside.
playbook.if_passing: None – no imminent bankruptcy or delisting has been flagged. The primary risk is a prolonged flat or further decline.

ai_insights.bottom_line: With a deep crash, dead sentiment and a fresh equity push, CHSS.L offers a high‑reward, low‑downside contrarian play that could rebound to 50% of its peak once the new capital fuels growth.
